display.purpose: The single headline metric on the Dashboard. Used exactly once per product.

title.purpose: Page-level headers across the app shell.

body.purpose: Descriptions, signal bodies, reasoning text; prose caps at 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Every micro-label: phase indicators, stat captions, field labels, status badges.

shadows.name: ring-rest
shadows.purpose: Default container edge — flat surface, no drop shadow.

shadows.name: ring-selected
shadows.purpose: Hover/selected state for the same containers — ring brightens to Field Sage.

shadows.name: ambient-float
shadows.purpose: Reserved for standalone floating list cards (Subreddit cards only), paired with a 4px backdrop blur.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Card/step entrance easing (onboarding stagger, auth card spring-in).

motion.name: drift-60s
motion.purpose: Slow background-texture drift on the Topographic Field.

motion.name: pulse-slow-2s
motion.purpose: The 'actively listening' pulsing accent dot used across nav, phase tracker, and status indicators.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Inverted-fill high-emphasis action, one per screen.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: var(--foreground, #eae7e0); color: var(--background, #0a1211); height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; border-radius: 0.535rem; border: none; font-family: var(--font-brand-sans, Outfit, sans-serif); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.8; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(74,106,94,0.5); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary action — transparent fill, hairline border.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { background: transparent; color: var(--foreground, #eae7e0); border: 1px solid var(--border, #2a413a); height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; border-radius: 0.535rem; font-family: var(--font-brand-sans, Outfit, sans-serif); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: rgba(42,65,58,0.4); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(74,106,94,0.5); }

components.name: Destructive Button
components.description: Soft tinted-wash destructive action — never a solid alert fill.
components.css: .ds-btn-destructive { background: rgba(196,84,74,0.1); color: #c4544a; border: none; height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; border-radius: 0.535rem; font-family: var(--font-brand-sans, Outfit, sans-serif); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-btn-destructive:hover { background: rgba(196,84,74,0.2); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Bordered field — used in denser forms outside onboarding's underline style.
components.css: .ds-input { height: 32px; width: 100%; background: transparent; color: var(--foreground, #eae7e0); border: 1px solid var(--border, #2a413a); border-radius: 0.535rem; padding: 4px 10px; font-family: var(--font-brand-sans, Outfit, sans-serif); font-size: 0.875rem; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--muted-foreground, #c4c4c4); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--accent, #4a6a5e); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(74,106,94,0.5); }

components.name: Panel Card
components.description: The default grouped-content container: flat, edge-defined by a ring, no drop shadow.
components.html:
Product details

Core configuration for signal targeting.
components.css: .ds-card { background: var(--background, #0a1211); border-radius: 0.749rem; padding: 24px; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px var(--border, #2a413a); transition: box-shadow 0.3s ease-out; } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px var(--accent, #4a6a5e); } .ds-card-title { font-family: var(--font-brand-sans, Outfit, sans-serif); font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 500; color: var(--foreground, #eae7e0); margin: 0 0 4px; } .ds-card-body { font-family: var(--font-brand-mono, 'Source Code Pro', monospace); font-size: 0.6875rem; letter-spacing: 0.15em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--muted-foreground, #c4c4c4); margin: 0; }

components.name: Switch Toggle
components.css: .ds-switch { width: 32px; height: 18.4px; border-radius: 9999px; background: var(--foreground, #eae7e0); border: none; padding: 2px; cursor: pointer; position: relative; transition: background-color 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-switch[aria-checked="false"] { background: var(--input, #2a413a); } .ds-switch-thumb { display: block; width: 14.4px; height: 14.4px; border-radius: 9999px; background: var(--background, #0a1211); transform: translateX(calc(100% - 2px)); transition: transform 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-switch[aria-checked="false"] .ds-switch-thumb { transform: translateX(0); background: var(--foreground, #eae7e0); }

components.name: Mono Label
components.description: The system's signature micro-label device — used for every field label, stat caption, phase indicator, and status badge. Functional, never a decorative section eyebrow.
components.css: .ds-mono-label { font-family: var(--font-brand-mono, 'Source Code Pro', monospace); font-size: 0.625rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.15em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--accent, #4a6a5e); }

components.name: Phase Tracker
components.description: Vertical stepper for multi-step flows (onboarding today; This Week / Outreach candidates later). A thin rule with a Field-Sage fill grown to the active step, a pulsing dot per active phase, mono-label captions.
components.html:
• Phase 01Establish context
  • Phase 02Positioning
  • Phase 03Node calibration
components.css: .ds-phase-tracker { list-style: none; margin: 0; padding: 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 40px; position: relative; } .ds-phase { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 20px; } .ds-phase-dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 9999px; background: var(--border, #2a413a); margin-top: 4px; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-phase.is-done .ds-phase-dot, .ds-phase.is-active .ds-phase-dot { background: var(--accent, #4a6a5e); } .ds-phase.is-active .ds-phase-dot { animation: ds-pulse 2s ease-in-out infinite; } @keyframes ds-pulse { 0%, 100% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(74,106,94,0.4); } 50% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 6px rgba(74,106,94,0); } } .ds-phase-title { display: block; font-family: var(--font-brand-sans, Outfit, sans-serif); font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; color: var(--foreground, #eae7e0); margin-top: 4px; }

narrative.overview: Earshot's interface doesn't perform "AI tool" — it performs *instrument*. Every surface reads like a quiet, always-on receiver: a near-black deep-teal field, a single warm-white signal, one muted sage accent that only lights up for what matters right now. The vocabulary is already load-bearing in the app's own copy — "listening node," "phase," "calibration," "signal" — and the visual system exists to make that vocabulary feel earned rather than decorative. This is not a SaaS dashboard wearing sci-fi skin; it's a precision instrument a stressed, time-constrained founder can trust to be watching carefully so they don't have to. The system explicitly rejects the generic SaaS-cream dashboard, any playful or cutesy startup register, and stodgy navy-and-gray enterprise blandness. Earshot is dark, quiet, and precise — never loud, never cute, never beige.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black deep-teal field with exactly one warm-white ink and one muted-sage accent — nothing else competes for attention.
Flat surfaces, edge-defined by a hairline inset ring rather than drop-shadow elevation.
A single recurring typographic device — the tiny tracked-out monospace uppercase label — does the work five different UI patterns would otherwise need.
Motion is diagnostic, not decorative: pulses, scan-lines, and phase-progress fills all read as "the system is actively listening," never as flourish.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Field Sage is the only saturated color in the system. If a screen needs a second visual emphasis, reach for weight or size on Signal White before reaching for a second hue.

rules.name: The Soft Alert Rule
rules.body: Destructive/error states are a tinted wash of Clay Alert (background at 10-20% opacity, full-opacity text) — never a solid red fill. Alarm is legible, not loud.

rules.name: The Mono Label Rule
rules.body: Any text under 12px is Source Code Pro, uppercase, and tracked at 0.15em, full stop. This is the system's single most-repeated device. It is a functional labeling convention, not a decorative marketing eyebrow.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Outfit carries every weight from body copy to the 11rem hero number. No second display face. Contrast comes from scale and the mono-label voice, not a second serif or humanist pairing.

rules.name: The Ring-Not-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Default elevation is a 1px inset border-ring, not a drop shadow. If a new panel needs to signal 'this is a container,' reach for the ring first; a floating shadow is the deliberate exception for standalone list cards, not the norm.

dos: Do keep Field Sage as the only saturated color on any given screen — it marks the one active/selected/emphasized thing, nothing else.
Do use the 1px inset ring (Ring-rest → Ring-selected) as the default way to define a container's edge; reach for a drop shadow only for a standalone floating list card.
Do set any label, caption, or status text under 12px in Source Code Pro, uppercase, 0.15em tracking — this is the system's signature device.
Do use the Soft Alert treatment (tinted wash, not solid fill) for every destructive/error state.
Do reuse the Phase Tracker pattern for any new multi-step flow rather than inventing a second stepper.
donts: Don't introduce a second accent hue. If a screen feels like it needs more color, that's a hierarchy problem to solve with weight/size/space, not a new hue.

Don't ship the generic SaaS-cream dashboard look — warm off-white surfaces, identical icon-heading-text card grids, or a gradient hero-metric block. This system is dark, quiet, and precise by explicit design decision, not by default.

Don't use the tiny mono-uppercase label as a decorative per-section marketing eyebrow ("ABOUT / PROCESS / PRICING" above every block). Its job here is functional labeling; reintroducing it as landing-page scaffolding is a different, banned pattern.
Don't use a solid, full-opacity Clay Alert fill for any destructive button or banner — always the tinted wash.
Don't add mascots, illustration, or exclamation-heavy copy anywhere in the product; the tone is a capable, calm instrument, not a consumer toy.
Don't reach for a corporate navy-and-gray enterprise treatment when a new B2B-feeling surface is added — stay inside the Void Teal / Signal White / Field Sage palette.
